description: failure_reason for max-attempts lease exhaustion is the queue's literal column value 'lease expired without heartbeat' rather than a synthesized 'max_attempts_exceeded'.
verifier_proposed_fix: Modify QueueDB.reclaim_expired() to write failure_reason='max_attempts_exceeded' when transitioning to timed_out, and update the assertion accordingly.
resolution_reason: The test asserts the queue's actual production contract (failure_reason='lease expired without heartbeat') established and verified twice in Set 1. Changing it would require modifying queue_db.py from set 001 - already shipped, already cross-provider VERIFIED twice (sessions 1 and 5 of set 001) - which lies outside the scope of set 002 session 4 and is not a Critical or Major regression. The verifier acknowledged the current value is 'technically true'. The state column ('timed_out') already encodes 'max_attempts_exceeded' semantics; the failure_reason describes the immediate cause (lease expiry without heartbeat). Surfacing for human review per workflow disagreement protocol; can be revisited at Step 9 reorganization review or as a Set 1 follow-up.

description: Provider name 'openai' is hardcoded in multiple test bodies.
resolution_reason: Applied: introduced TEST_PROVIDER = 'openai' near the top of the file and replaced 17 inline 'openai' uses inside test bodies (left the comment in the constant's docstring intact for context).

description: Scenario 6 docstring did not call out that the test exercises the static-configuration form of the spec's 'drift' narrative.
verifier_proposed_fix: Add a leading paragraph stating the deviation and why the static-config form is the practical interpretation.
resolution_reason: Applied: prepended a paragraph describing the spec's drift framing and noting that the drift case degrades to the static-config case because no enqueue path exists when validate_mode_config rejects the spec.
